matrix.title: Untitled
matrix.research_problem: The ever-increasing number of research articles makes manual literature review time-consuming, tedious, and error-prone. The primary objective is to develop a system that can automatically generate literature review segments from PDF files using NLP techniques and LLMs.
matrix.method_summary: Three approaches are implemented and compared: (1) frequency-based approach using spaCy for extractive summarization, (2) transformer-based Simple T5 model for abstractive summarization trained on SciTLDR, and (3) retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) using GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with the SciTLDR dataset as knowledge base.
matrix.datasets: SciTLDR dataset (Hugging Face) - 5,400 TLDRs from over 3,200 papers containing abstracts, introductions, conclusions, and summaries
matrix.claim_text: The LLM-based approach (GPT-3.5-turbo) achieved the highest ROUGE-1 score of 0.364, outperforming T5 (0.268) and spaCy (0.257)
matrix.limitations: Models are not yet fully capable of automating literature reviews independently and require human reviewers | The research uses only the SciTLDR dataset, limiting generalizability to other domains | The spaCy approach has the lowest performance among the three methods with ROUGE-1 of 0.257
matrix.notes: First procedure uses spaCy library with word frequency calculation for sentence weighting; Second procedure uses Simple T5 model fine-tuned on SciTLDR dataset; Third procedure uses GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with RAG approach and custom prompt engineering; Final system includes a GUI where users can upload multiple PDFs and receive generated literature review; User evaluation was performed with 4 IoT/Healthcare research articles demonstrating coherent output generation

matrix.title: Untitled
matrix.research_problem: The ever-increasing number of research articles makes manual literature review time-consuming, tedious, and error-prone. The primary objective is to develop a system that can automatically generate literature review segments from PDF files using NLP techniques and LLMs.
matrix.method_summary: Three approaches are implemented and compared: (1) frequency-based approach using spaCy for extractive summarization, (2) transformer-based Simple T5 model for abstractive summarization trained on SciTLDR, and (3) retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) using GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with the SciTLDR dataset as knowledge base.
matrix.datasets: SciTLDR dataset (Hugging Face) - 5,400 TLDRs from over 3,200 papers containing abstracts, introductions, conclusions, and summaries
matrix.claim_text: All three NLP approaches (spaCy, T5, and GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125) can produce satisfactory results in automating literature review generation
matrix.limitations: Models are not yet fully capable of automating literature reviews independently and require human reviewers | The research uses only the SciTLDR dataset, limiting generalizability to other domains | The spaCy approach has the lowest performance among the three methods with ROUGE-1 of 0.257
matrix.notes: First procedure uses spaCy library with word frequency calculation for sentence weighting; Second procedure uses Simple T5 model fine-tuned on SciTLDR dataset; Third procedure uses GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with RAG approach and custom prompt engineering; Final system includes a GUI where users can upload multiple PDFs and receive generated literature review; User evaluation was performed with 4 IoT/Healthcare research articles demonstrating coherent output generation

matrix.title: Untitled
matrix.research_problem: The ever-increasing number of research articles makes manual literature review time-consuming, tedious, and error-prone. The primary objective is to develop a system that can automatically generate literature review segments from PDF files using NLP techniques and LLMs.
matrix.method_summary: Three approaches are implemented and compared: (1) frequency-based approach using spaCy for extractive summarization, (2) transformer-based Simple T5 model for abstractive summarization trained on SciTLDR, and (3) retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) using GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with the SciTLDR dataset as knowledge base.
matrix.datasets: SciTLDR dataset (Hugging Face) - 5,400 TLDRs from over 3,200 papers containing abstracts, introductions, conclusions, and summaries
matrix.claim_text: The transformer-based T5 model ranks second in performance with ROUGE-1 of 0.268, while spaCy ranks last with ROUGE-1 of 0.257
matrix.limitations: Models are not yet fully capable of automating literature reviews independently and require human reviewers | The research uses only the SciTLDR dataset, limiting generalizability to other domains | The spaCy approach has the lowest performance among the three methods with ROUGE-1 of 0.257
matrix.notes: First procedure uses spaCy library with word frequency calculation for sentence weighting; Second procedure uses Simple T5 model fine-tuned on SciTLDR dataset; Third procedure uses GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with RAG approach and custom prompt engineering; Final system includes a GUI where users can upload multiple PDFs and receive generated literature review; User evaluation was performed with 4 IoT/Healthcare research articles demonstrating coherent output generation

matrix.title: Untitled
matrix.research_problem: The ever-increasing number of research articles makes manual literature review time-consuming, tedious, and error-prone. The primary objective is to develop a system that can automatically generate literature review segments from PDF files using NLP techniques and LLMs.
matrix.method_summary: Three approaches are implemented and compared: (1) frequency-based approach using spaCy for extractive summarization, (2) transformer-based Simple T5 model for abstractive summarization trained on SciTLDR, and (3) retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) using GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with the SciTLDR dataset as knowledge base.
matrix.datasets: SciTLDR dataset (Hugging Face) - 5,400 TLDRs from over 3,200 papers containing abstracts, introductions, conclusions, and summaries
matrix.claim_text: Large language models are proven superior to older NLP approaches for literature review generation based on ROUGE-N scores
matrix.limitations: Models are not yet fully capable of automating literature reviews independently and require human reviewers | The research uses only the SciTLDR dataset, limiting generalizability to other domains | The spaCy approach has the lowest performance among the three methods with ROUGE-1 of 0.257
matrix.notes: First procedure uses spaCy library with word frequency calculation for sentence weighting; Second procedure uses Simple T5 model fine-tuned on SciTLDR dataset; Third procedure uses GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with RAG approach and custom prompt engineering; Final system includes a GUI where users can upload multiple PDFs and receive generated literature review; User evaluation was performed with 4 IoT/Healthcare research articles demonstrating coherent output generation

automated_literature_review_generation.claim_text: The LLM-based approach (GPT-3.5-turbo) achieved the highest ROUGE-1 score of 0.364, outperforming T5 (0.268) and spaCy (0.257)
All three NLP approaches (spaCy, T5, and GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125) can produce satisfactory results in automating literature review generation
The transformer-based T5 model ranks second in performance with ROUGE-1 of 0.268, while spaCy ranks last with ROUGE-1 of 0.257
Large language models are proven superior to older NLP approaches for literature review generation based on ROUGE-N scores

gaps.gap_statement: Current coverage appears to underrepresent Chinese-language or Chinese-context studies.
There are unresolved claim differences across papers under the same task, suggesting a need for clearer comparison criteria.
